shadcn-html / native-web-apis

Native Web APIs

If the browser can do it, we don't write JavaScript for it. shadcn-html builds on modern browser APIs instead of third-party libraries.

The native-first principle

Traditional component libraries ship thousands of lines of JavaScript to implement behaviors that browsers now provide natively — dropdown positioning, modal focus trapping, popover light-dismiss, auto-growing textareas. shadcn-html eliminates that JavaScript entirely by building on the platform.

Traditional approach 
<!-- Dropdown: Floating UI + JS toggle
     + click-outside + focus trap
     + scroll lock + virtual positioning -->

<div class="dropdown-trigger" onclick="...">
  Open Menu
</div>
<div class="dropdown-panel"
  style="position:absolute;top:...;left:...">
  ...
</div>
shadcn-html approach 
<!-- Popover API + CSS Anchor Positioning
     Browser handles: positioning, light-dismiss,
     top-layer, and auto-flip -->

<button popovertarget="menu">
  Open Menu
</button>
<div id="menu" popover
  class="dropdown-content">
  ...
</div>

Popover API

The Popover API provides native show/hide behavior with light-dismiss (click outside to close), top-layer rendering (no z-index wars), and keyboard dismiss (Escape key) — all without JavaScript.

MDN: Popover API

popover HTML attribute that makes any element a popover. Renders in the top layer when shown. popovertarget On a button, specifies the ID of the popover to toggle. No JavaScript needed for basic show/hide. .showPopover() JS method to programmatically show a popover (used for keyboard-triggered menus). .hidePopover() JS method to programmatically hide a popover. :popover-open CSS pseudo-class that matches when the popover is visible. Used for enter/exit animations. toggle event Fires when a popover opens or closes. e.newState is "open" or "closed".
basic popover — zero JavaScript 
<button class="btn" popovertarget="my-popover">
  Open Popover
</button>

<div id="my-popover" popover class="popover">
  <p>This popover uses the native Popover API.</p>
  <p>Click outside or press Escape to close.</p>
</div>

Used by: Popover, Dropdown, Tooltip, Toast, Combobox, Context Menu, Navigation Menu

CSS Anchor Positioning

CSS Anchor Positioning lets a popover attach itself to a trigger element entirely in CSS — no Floating UI, no Popper.js, no JavaScript positioning calculations. The browser automatically handles viewport-edge flipping with position-try-fallbacks.

MDN: CSS Anchor Positioning

anchor-name CSS property (or set via JS) on the trigger. Creates a named anchor: anchor-name: --my-anchor. position-anchor On the positioned element. Points to the named anchor: position-anchor: --my-anchor. anchor() CSS function to reference anchor edges: top: anchor(bottom) positions the popup below the trigger. position-try-fallbacks Automatic flip when the popup would overflow the viewport: position-try-fallbacks: flip-block.
how it works in CSS 
/* Component CSS — no JS positioning needed */
.popover {
  position: fixed;
  inset: auto;
  top: anchor(bottom);
  left: anchor(left);
  margin-top: 4px;
  position-try-fallbacks: flip-block;
}

/* JS only sets the anchor names (unique per instance) */
JS anchor name setup 
// Each trigger-popover pair gets a unique anchor ID
trigger.style.anchorName = `--popover-${id}`;
popover.style.positionAnchor = `--popover-${id}`;

Used by: Dropdown, Popover, Tooltip, Combobox, Navigation Menu

<dialog> Element

The <dialog> element provides native modal behavior: focus trapping, Escape-to-close, ::backdrop overlay, and top-layer rendering — all built into the browser.

MDN: <dialog>

.showModal() Opens the dialog as a modal — traps focus, adds backdrop, blocks interaction with the rest of the page. .close() Closes the dialog. The browser handles cleanup and fires the close event. ::backdrop CSS pseudo-element for the overlay behind the modal. Style it directly — no extra div needed. Escape key Browsers close modal dialogs on Escape automatically. No keyboard listener needed.
dialog pattern 
<button class="btn" data-dialog-trigger="my-dialog">
  Open Dialog
</button>

<dialog id="my-dialog" class="dialog">
  <div class="dialog-content">
    <h2 class="dialog-title">Dialog Title</h2>
    <p class="dialog-description">Dialog content here.</p>
    <button class="btn" data-dialog-close>Close</button>
  </div>
</dialog>

Used by: Dialog, Alert Dialog, Sheet

<details> / <summary>

The <details> element provides native disclosure (expand/collapse) behavior. The browser handles toggle state, keyboard activation, and accessibility — no JavaScript needed for basic accordions.

MDN: <details>

open Boolean attribute that toggles the expanded state. CSS can style with details[open]. ::details-content Pseudo-element for the collapsible content area. Enables block-size animation for smooth open/close. toggle event Fires when the details element opens or closes.
accordion using <details> 
<div class="accordion">
  <details class="accordion-item">
    <summary class="accordion-trigger">
      Section Title
      <svg class="accordion-chevron">...</svg>
    </summary>
    <div class="accordion-content">
      <p>Collapsible content here.</p>
    </div>
  </details>
</div>

Used by: Accordion, Collapsible

field-sizing: content

The field-sizing: content CSS property makes textareas automatically grow and shrink to fit their content — zero JavaScript, no resize observers, no measuring hidden elements.

MDN: field-sizing

auto-growing textarea — CSS only 
textarea.input {
  field-sizing: content;
  min-height: 5rem;
}

Used by: Textarea

:has() Selector

The :has() selector lets CSS react to child or sibling state without JavaScript class propagation. A parent can style itself based on what its children are doing.

MDN: :has()

examples from the component library 
/* Tabs detect vertical orientation from child */
.tabs:has(.tab-list[aria-orientation="vertical"]) {
  display: flex;
}

/* Avatar hides fallback when image loads */
.avatar:has(.avatar-image:not([data-error])) .avatar-fallback {
  display: none;
}

Used by: Label, Tabs, Avatar

Container Queries

Container queries let components adapt to their own container width rather than the viewport. This makes components truly portable — they respond to the space they're given, not the screen size.

MDN: Container queries

component-level responsive design 
/* Card becomes a containment context */
.card {
  container-type: inline-size;
}

/* Card adapts to its own width, not the viewport */
@container (max-width: 280px) {
  .card-header { padding: 0.5rem; }
}

Used by: Card, Table

Invoker Commands API

The Invoker Commands API (commandfor / command) lets buttons declaratively control dialogs, popovers, and other interactive elements — no JavaScript click handlers needed. The browser handles showModal(), togglePopover(), and close() automatically.

MDN: Invoker Commands API

commandfor HTML attribute on a <button>. Points to the ID of the element to control. command Specifies the action: show-modal, close, toggle-popover, show-popover, hide-popover. CommandEvent Fires on the target element. Use custom commands (--my-action) for app-specific behavior.
declarative dialog trigger — zero JavaScript 
<!-- No JS click handler needed -->
<button class="btn" commandfor="my-dialog" command="show-modal">
  Open Dialog
</button>

<dialog id="my-dialog" class="dialog">
  <div class="dialog-content">
    <h2 class="dialog-title">Title</h2>
    <button class="btn" commandfor="my-dialog" command="close">
      Close
    </button>
  </div>
</dialog>

Used by: Dialog, Alert Dialog, Sheet, Popover

@starting-style & Discrete Transitions

@starting-style defines the initial state for enter animations on elements transitioning from display: none. Combined with transition-behavior: allow-discrete, it enables smooth enter and exit animations on dialogs, popovers, and tooltips — entirely in CSS, no JavaScript class toggling.

MDN: @starting-style

@starting-style At-rule defining initial property values before the first frame of an enter animation. transition-behavior: allow-discrete Allows display and overlay to participate in transitions — enabling exit animations.
enter/exit animations — CSS only 
dialog.dialog {
  opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.98);
  transition: opacity 150ms, transform 150ms,
              display 150ms allow-discrete;

  &[open] { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
}

/* Enter animation starts from these values */
@starting-style {
  dialog.dialog[open] {
    opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.98);
  }
}

Used by: Dialog, Alert Dialog, Sheet, Dropdown, Tooltip, Command, Accordion

oklch() & Relative Color Syntax

All design tokens use oklch() for perceptually uniform, wide-gamut colors. Relative color syntax (oklch(from var(--color) l c h / alpha)) derives focus rings, hover states, and alpha variants directly from token values — no separate color definitions needed.

MDN: oklch()

derive colors from tokens with relative syntax 
/* Focus ring with 20% opacity — derived from token */
.input:focus {
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(from var(--ring) l c h / 0.2);
}

/* Destructive focus ring — same pattern, different token */
.input:user-invalid:focus {
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(from var(--destructive) l c h / 0.2);
}

Used by: All design tokens, Input, Button, and any component with focus/hover states

color-mix()

color-mix() blends two colors in a specified color space. Combined with oklch, it produces perceptually uniform mixes — useful for hover states, borders, and disabled variations derived from token values without hardcoding new colors.

MDN: color-mix()

derive hover/disabled states from tokens 
/* 90% primary + 10% background → subtle hover */
.btn:hover {
  background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 90%, var(--background));
}

/* 50% mix → disabled appearance from existing tokens */
.btn:disabled {
  background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 50%, var(--muted));
}

Useful for: Button hover/disabled states, border variations, tinted backgrounds

light-dark()

The light-dark() CSS function returns one of two values based on the element's computed color-scheme. It eliminates the need for media queries or class toggles when a single property needs different values in light and dark modes.

MDN: light-dark()

inline dark mode — no media query needed 
/* Requires color-scheme to be set (already in tokens) */
:root { color-scheme: light dark; }

.code-block {
  background: light-dark(oklch(0.97 0 0), oklch(0.2 0 0));
  border-color: light-dark(oklch(0.9 0 0), oklch(0.3 0 0));
}

Useful for: Inline color switching anywhere color-scheme is set, hardcoded values that need dark variants

prefers-reduced-motion

The prefers-reduced-motion media query detects when a user has requested reduced animation in their OS settings. This is an accessibility requirement — users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity rely on it. All @starting-style transitions, keyframe animations, and el.animate() calls should be suppressed or simplified when this preference is active.

MDN: prefers-reduced-motion

global animation suppression 
/* Remove all transitions and animations system-wide */
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *, *::before, *::after, ::backdrop {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}

/* Or suppress per-component */
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  dialog.dialog { transition: none; }
}
JavaScript — check before animating 
const motionOk = !matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;
if (motionOk) {
  el.animate([{ opacity: 0 }, { opacity: 1 }], { duration: 200 });
}

Should be used by: All components with transitions or animations (Dialog, Sheet, Dropdown, Tooltip, Accordion, Toast, Carousel, Command)

prefers-color-scheme

The prefers-color-scheme media query detects the user's OS-level dark/light preference. While the design system uses a .dark class toggle for explicit control, this media query enables automatic dark mode as the default fallback — respecting user preference without requiring JavaScript interaction.

MDN: prefers-color-scheme

automatic dark mode from OS preference 
/* Opt-in: auto-apply dark tokens when OS is dark */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  :root:not(.light) {
    color-scheme: dark;
    --background: oklch(0.1450 0 0);
    --foreground: oklch(0.9850 0 0);
    /* ... remaining dark tokens */
  }
}

Useful for: Automatic dark mode, image/media switching, system-aware defaults

prefers-contrast & forced-colors

prefers-contrast detects when a user has requested higher or lower contrast in their OS. forced-colors detects Windows High Contrast Mode, where the browser overrides all colors with system colors. Both are critical for accessibility compliance — components must remain usable when these are active.

MDN: prefers-contrast · MDN: forced-colors

high contrast & forced-colors support 
/* Increase contrast when requested */
@media (prefers-contrast: more) {
  .btn { border: 2px solid currentColor; }
}

/* Windows High Contrast Mode — use system colors */
@media (forced-colors: active) {
  .btn {
    border: 1px solid ButtonText;
    background: ButtonFace;
    color: ButtonText;
  }
  .btn:focus-visible {
    outline: 2px solid Highlight;
  }
}

Should be used by: All interactive components — ensures usability in Windows High Contrast Mode and high-contrast settings

<progress> & <meter> Elements

The <progress> element represents task completion (determinate or indeterminate). The <meter> element represents a scalar value within a known range (e.g., disk usage, password strength). Both provide built-in semantics for assistive technology and can be styled with pseudo-elements.

MDN: <progress> · MDN: <meter>

value / max Set completion percentage. Omit value for indeterminate state (spinner-style). :indeterminate CSS pseudo-class matching <progress> without a value attribute — animate for loading states. ::-webkit-progress-bar Styles the track (background) of the progress element in Chromium/Safari. ::-moz-progress-bar Styles the fill (value indicator) of the progress element in Firefox. low / high / optimum <meter> attributes that define value ranges. The browser changes color (green/yellow/red) automatically.
semantic progress & meter 
<!-- Determinate progress bar -->
<progress class="progress" value="60" max="100"></progress>

<!-- Indeterminate (loading) -->
<progress class="progress"></progress>

<!-- Meter with auto-coloring ranges -->
<meter min="0" max="100" low="25" high="75"
       optimum="80" value="90">90%</meter>

Used by: Progress. Useful for: Meter, password strength indicators, storage usage

<output> Element

The <output> element represents the result of a calculation or user action. It has an implicit aria-live="polite" role, meaning assistive technology automatically announces changes — no manual ARIA attributes or live region setup needed.

MDN: <output>

accessible computed output 
<!-- Slider value display — auto-announced by screen readers -->
<label for="amount">Donation</label>
<input type="range" id="amount" name="amount" min="0" max="100">
<output for="amount">50</output>

Useful for: Slider value displays, form totals, live calculation results, search result counts

View Transitions API

The View Transitions API creates smooth animated transitions between DOM states. For same-document transitions, document.startViewTransition() captures old and new visual states and crossfades them automatically. For cross-document (MPA) transitions, the @view-transition at-rule enables transitions between page navigations.

MDN: View Transitions API

view-transition-name CSS property that tags an element for independent transition animation (morph effect between states). startViewTransition() JS method that captures the old state, runs the callback (DOM update), then animates to the new state. ::view-transition-old / ::view-transition-new Pseudo-elements for styling the outgoing and incoming snapshots during a transition. @view-transition At-rule enabling cross-document transitions in multi-page apps: @view-transition { navigation: auto; }.
smooth DOM state change 
// Wrap any DOM mutation in a view transition
document.startViewTransition(() => {
  list.innerHTML = newContent;
});

Useful for: SPA page transitions, tab content switches, list reordering, dark mode toggles, hero image morphs

Scroll-Driven Animations

Scroll-driven animations bind CSS animations or Web Animations to scroll progress instead of time. scroll() tracks a scroll container's position. view() tracks an element's visibility within a scrollport. Both enable scroll-linked effects like progress bars, parallax, and reveal animations — entirely in CSS, no IntersectionObserver or scroll listeners needed.

MDN: animation-timeline · MDN: scroll()

animation-timeline: scroll() Links animation progress to scroll position of a container. animation-timeline: view() Links animation progress to an element's visibility within a scrollport — CSS-only reveal animations. animation-range Controls when the animation starts and ends within the scroll/view timeline (e.g., entry 0% cover 50%).
scroll-linked progress indicator — CSS only 
/* Reading progress bar at top of page */
.scroll-progress {
  position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0;
  width: 100%; height: 3px;
  background: var(--primary);
  transform-origin: left;
  animation: grow-width linear;
  animation-timeline: scroll();
}
@keyframes grow-width { from { scale: 0 1; } to { scale: 1 1; } }

/* Fade-in on scroll into view */
.reveal {
  animation: fade-in linear both;
  animation-timeline: view();
  animation-range: entry 0% entry 100%;
}
@keyframes fade-in { from { opacity: 0; } to { opacity: 1; } }

Useful for: Reading progress indicators, scroll-reveal animations, parallax effects, sticky header transitions

interpolate-size: allow-keywords

CSS cannot normally transition to or from intrinsic sizes like auto, min-content, or max-content. The interpolate-size: allow-keywords property enables these transitions — making smooth height/width animations to auto possible without JavaScript measurement or max-height hacks.

MDN: interpolate-size

animate to auto height — no JS measurement 
/* Enable globally or per-component */
:root { interpolate-size: allow-keywords; }

.accordion-content {
  height: 0;
  overflow: hidden;
  transition: height 300ms ease;
}
details[open] > .accordion-content {
  height: auto; /* Now animates smoothly! */
}

Useful for: Accordion, Collapsible, expanding search bars, sidebar collapse, any height-to-auto animation

@property

@property registers CSS custom properties with types, inheritance rules, and initial values. This unlocks transitions and animations on custom properties (e.g. animating --progress from 0 to 1), enables type checking, and provides fallback values at the CSS engine level.

MDN: @property

animate a custom property 
@property --progress {
  syntax: '<number>';
  inherits: false;
  initial-value: 0;
}

.progress-ring {
  --progress: 0;
  background: conic-gradient(var(--primary) calc(var(--progress) * 100%), transparent 0);
  transition: --progress 600ms ease;
}
.progress-ring[data-complete] { --progress: 1; }

Useful for: Animated progress rings, gradient transitions, typed design tokens with engine-level validation

inert Attribute

The inert HTML attribute makes an entire subtree non-interactive — not focusable, not clickable, hidden from assistive technology. <dialog>.showModal() applies this automatically to the rest of the page, but inert is also useful for custom overlays, skeleton/loading states, and off-screen content like sidebar panels.

MDN: inert

disable interaction on background content 
<!-- Content behind a custom overlay -->
<main inert>
  <!-- All links, buttons, inputs become non-interactive -->
</main>
<div class="custom-overlay">...</div>

<!-- Collapsed sidebar — prevent hidden content from being focusable -->
<aside class="sidebar" inert>...</aside>

Useful for: Custom overlays, collapsed sidebars, loading/skeleton states, off-screen panels, wizard step content

Exclusive Accordion (name attribute)

The name attribute on <details> elements creates an exclusive accordion — only one panel in the group can be open at a time. When a user opens one, the browser automatically closes the others. Zero JavaScript needed for single-expansion behavior.

MDN: details name attribute

single-open accordion — zero JavaScript 
<div class="accordion">
  <details class="accordion-item" name="faq">
    <summary>Question 1</summary>
    <div class="accordion-content">Answer 1</div>
  </details>
  <details class="accordion-item" name="faq">
    <summary>Question 2</summary>
    <div class="accordion-content">Answer 2</div>
  </details>
</div>

Used by: Accordion (single-expand mode)

popover="hint"

The hint popover type is designed for tooltip-like UI — it doesn't close other auto-popovers when shown, and multiple hints can coexist. A popover="hint" only dismisses other hints, making it the correct semantic type for hover-triggered descriptive text.

MDN: Using hint popover

hint popover for tooltips 
<!-- Won't close open dropdowns or menus -->
<button popovertarget="tip" popovertargetaction="show">
  Hover me
</button>

<div id="tip" popover="hint" class="tooltip">
  Helpful description text
</div>

Useful for: Tooltip, descriptive hover text that shouldn't interfere with menus/dropdowns

HTML Elements & Attributes

Semantic HTML elements, attributes, and browser APIs that provide built-in behavior — no JavaScript needed for the basics.

Element / AttributeWhat it doesReplaces
<dialog> Native modal with focus trapping, Escape-to-close, ::backdrop, and top-layer rendering JS modal + overlay + focus trap
<details> / <summary> Native disclosure (expand/collapse) with keyboard activation and built-in toggle state JS toggle + height animation
<details name> Groups <details> elements into an exclusive accordion — only one can be open at a time JS single-expand accordion logic
<progress> Semantic task completion indicator with :indeterminate state and styleable pseudo-elements Custom div-based progress bars
<meter> Scalar value within a known range — auto-colors based on low/high/optimum attributes Custom gauge/indicator components
<output> Result of a calculation or user action — implicit aria-live="polite" for auto-announcement Manual aria-live regions + <span>
popover attribute Makes any element a popover — top-layer rendering, light-dismiss, Escape-to-close, no z-index wars JS show/hide + z-index management + click-outside
popover="hint" Tooltip-type popover that doesn't close other auto-popovers — multiple hints can coexist Custom tooltip z-index management
popovertarget / popovertargetaction Declarative popover toggle/show/hide from a button — no JS click handler needed JS togglePopover() click handlers
commandfor / command Declarative button→element control: show-modal, close, toggle-popover, custom commands JS click handlers calling showModal()/togglePopover()
inert Makes an entire subtree non-interactive — not focusable, not clickable, hidden from assistive tech JS focus trap + aria-hidden toggling
autofocus Automatically focuses an element when shown — works with dialogs and popovers natively JS .focus() calls in open handlers
inputmode Controls the virtual keyboard type on mobile — numeric, email, tel, search, url Separate input types or JS keyboard detection
enterkeyhint Customizes the Enter key label on mobile keyboards — search, send, go, next, done No equivalent — mobile-only UX gap
loading="lazy" Native lazy loading for <img> and <iframe> — defers until near viewport IntersectionObserver + JS lazy load libraries
fetchpriority Hints resource priority to the browser: high for hero images, low for below-fold assets Manual preload/prefetch link elements
autocomplete Hints to the browser what value to autofill — name, email, tel, address-line1, cc-number Custom autofill libraries
list + <datalist> Native type-ahead suggestions for inputs — browser renders the dropdown, no JS needed Custom autocomplete/combobox from scratch
form attribute Associates an input with a form by ID — input can be anywhere in the DOM, not nested inside <form> JS to manually collect values from outside a form
formmethod / formaction Override the parent form's method or action per-button — one form, multiple submit endpoints JS click handlers that change form attributes
disabled / readonly Native disabled (skipped in form submission) and readonly (included but not editable) states JS class toggling + manual validation skips

CSS Utility Features

Modern CSS properties and features used (or recommended) throughout the system to replace common JavaScript workarounds and build tools.

FeatureWhat it doesReplaces
:focus-visible Shows focus rings only for keyboard navigation, not mouse clicks JS focus-ring detection libraries
:user-valid / :user-invalid Validation styling that activates only after user interaction — not on page load JS blur listeners + class toggling
color-scheme Tells the browser to render native controls (scrollbars, inputs) in light or dark mode Manual dark overrides on every native element
text-wrap: balance Balances line lengths in headings so no line is much shorter than the others JS text-balancing libraries
overscroll-behavior: contain Prevents scroll chaining — scrolling inside a dropdown won't scroll the page behind it JS scroll-lock libraries
scroll-snap Snaps carousel slides to grid points during scroll JS snap-to-slide calculations
scrollbar-gutter: stable Reserves space for scrollbars to prevent layout shift when content overflows Padding hacks for scrollbar width
backdrop-filter: blur() Glass-morphism blur effect on ::backdrop overlays JS-managed overlay divs with canvas blur
Individual transforms rotate, scale, translate as separate properties that compose without overwriting Compound transform strings
CSS nesting / @layer Native selector nesting and cascade layer ordering — no build step Sass, Less, PostCSS preprocessors
accent-color Themes native form controls (checkboxes, radios, range sliders, progress bars) with a single property Custom-styled replacement elements
aspect-ratio Sets intrinsic aspect ratio on any element — images, videos, cards, containers Padding-bottom percentage hacks
content-visibility Skips rendering of off-screen content. Used with allow-discrete for smooth expand/collapse transitions JS lazy rendering, virtual scrolling
color-mix() Blends two colors in any color space — derive hover/disabled states from tokens Hardcoded color variants, Sass darken()/lighten()
:is() / :where() Selector list shorthand. :is() takes highest specificity; :where() has zero specificity (ideal for resets) Repeated selectors, specificity hacks
Logical properties margin-inline, padding-block, inset-inline-start, inline-size — direction-aware layout Separate LTR/RTL stylesheets
Subgrid grid-template-columns: subgrid — children align to parent grid tracks Manually synchronized column widths
@supports Feature detection in CSS — apply styles only when a property or value is supported Modernizr, JS feature detection
Dynamic viewport units dvh, svh, lvh — account for mobile browser chrome (address bar, toolbars) JS window.innerHeight hacks, CSS vh on mobile
text-wrap: pretty Prevents orphans in body text (last line with a single word). Pair with balance for headings Manual &nbsp; or JS word-wrap libraries
CSS math functions clamp(), min(), max(), round(), mod(), abs() — responsive sizing, clamped typography JS resize calculations, media query breakpoints
@scope Scoped styling with upper and lower boundaries: @scope (.card) to (.card-content) BEM naming, Shadow DOM, CSS modules
hanging-punctuation Optically aligns quotation marks and punctuation outside the text boundary Manual negative text-indent
@media (scripting) Detects whether JavaScript is enabled — progressive enhancement for no-JS fallbacks <noscript> element only

JavaScript APIs

When behavior requires JavaScript, we use native browser APIs — never third-party libraries. These are the platform APIs relevant to UI component development.

APIWhat it doesReplacesComponents
Web Animations API el.animate() for imperative, JS-coordinated animations with .finished promise CSS class toggling for sequenced animations Toast
Intl.DateTimeFormat Locale-aware date and time formatting with no external dependencies moment.js, date-fns Calendar
Drag and Drop API Native drag events (dragstart, dragover, drop) with dataTransfer SortableJS, drag libraries Sortable
CustomEvent Component-to-component communication via the DOM event system Framework event buses Calendar
checkVisibility() Detects if an element is visible in the DOM (handles display: none, visibility, etc.) Manual offset/dimension calculations Tree View
IntersectionObserver Observes when elements enter or leave the viewport — lazy loading, infinite scroll, reveal effects Scroll event listeners + getBoundingClientRect() Lazy images, infinite scroll lists
ResizeObserver Watches element size changes — trigger layout adjustments when containers resize Window resize listeners + polling Responsive components, Carousel
MutationObserver Watches DOM changes — react to dynamically added content (e.g., SPA re-initialization) Polling loops, framework lifecycle hooks Auto-init for dynamically added components
Clipboard API navigator.clipboard.writeText() / .readText() for async clipboard access document.execCommand('copy') Code blocks, copy buttons, share links
Intl.NumberFormat Locale-aware number formatting — currencies, percentages, compact notation (1.2K) numeral.js, manual formatting functions Statistic, Number Input, data displays
Intl.RelativeTimeFormat Locale-aware relative time strings ("3 days ago", "in 2 hours") moment.js .fromNow(), timeago.js Timeline, comments, activity feeds
Intl.ListFormat Locale-aware list formatting ("A, B, and C" vs. "A, B y C") Manual join/comma logic Tag lists, multi-select displays
CloseWatcher Handles platform close signals (Escape, Android back button) for custom UI that isn't <dialog> or popover Manual keydown Escape listeners Custom overlays, full-screen views
ElementInternals Custom elements participate in forms natively — validation, FormData, disabled state, labels Hidden <input> proxy patterns Custom form controls (Web Components)
FormData Serializes form inputs into key/value pairs for submission — works with native validation Manual querySelector loops to collect values Form, search, filter patterns
AbortController Cancels fetch requests, event listeners, or any async operation cleanly Boolean flags, manual cleanup Search-as-you-type, debounced fetches
structuredClone() Deep clones objects, arrays, Maps, Sets, Dates — no libraries needed JSON.parse(JSON.stringify()), lodash cloneDeep State management, undo/redo
Navigation API navigation.navigate() for SPA routing, NavigateEvent for intercepting navigations History API hacks, router libraries SPA router, pagination
Selection API window.getSelection() — detect and manipulate text selections programmatically Range/offset calculations Text editors, highlight/annotation features

API reference

Every native API available for the component library, what it replaces, and where it applies.

native APIs vs. traditional JS libraries
Native APIReplacesComponents
HTML Elements & Attributes
<dialog>JS modal + overlay + focus trapDialog, Alert Dialog, Sheet, Command
<details>/<summary>JS toggle + height animationAccordion, Collapsible
<details name> (exclusive)JS single-expand accordion logicAccordion (single mode)
<progress>Custom div-based progress barsProgress
<meter>Custom gauge componentsPassword strength, storage indicators
<output>Manual aria-live regionsSlider displays, form totals, live results
inertJS focus trap + aria-hidden togglingOverlays, collapsed sidebars, loading states
Popover APIJS show/hide, z-index management, click-outsidePopover, Dropdown, Tooltip, Toast, Combobox, Context Menu, Nav Menu
popover="hint"Custom tooltip z-index managementTooltip, descriptive hovers
Invoker Commands (commandfor/command)JS click handlers calling showModal()/togglePopover()Dialog, Alert Dialog, Sheet, Popover
CSS Layout & Positioning
CSS Anchor PositioningFloating UI, Popper.jsDropdown, Popover, Tooltip, Combobox, Nav Menu
Container QueriesViewport media queriesCard, Table
SubgridManually synchronized column widthsForm layouts, settings pages, description lists
Logical propertiesSeparate LTR/RTL stylesheetsAll components (RTL support)
Dynamic viewport units (dvh/svh/lvh)JS innerHeight hacks for mobile chromeFull-screen overlays, mobile layout
CSS Animations & Transitions
@starting-style + allow-discreteJS class toggling for enter/exit animationsAll overlays (Dialog, Sheet, Dropdown, Tooltip, Command, Accordion)
Individual transformsCompound transform stringsAccordion, composable animations
interpolate-size: allow-keywordsJS height measurement + max-height hacksAccordion, Collapsible, expanding UI
Scroll-driven animationsScroll listeners + IntersectionObserver for effectsProgress indicators, scroll reveals, parallax
View Transitions APIJS DOM-diffing + class-toggled crossfadesSPA navigation, tab switches, list reordering
@propertyJS-animated values, non-animatable custom propertiesProgress rings, gradient transitions
CSS Colors & Theming
oklch() + relative color syntaxHardcoded hex/hsl, manual color derivationAll tokens, all focus/hover states
color-mix()Sass darken()/lighten(), hardcoded variantsHover/disabled states, tinted backgrounds
light-dark()Media queries or class toggles for dark mode valuesInline light/dark switching
color-schemeManual dark overrides on native controlsDesign tokens (global)
accent-colorCustom-styled replacement form controlsSlider, Checkbox, Radio, Progress
CSS Selectors & Pseudo-classes
:has()JS class propagationLabel, Tabs, Avatar, Form
:focus-visibleJS focus-ring detectionAll interactive components
:user-valid / :user-invalidJS blur + class toggling for validationInput, Textarea, Select, Checkbox
:is() / :where()Repeated selectors, specificity hacksComponent resets, grouped selectors
::backdrop + backdrop-filterJS-managed overlay divs, canvas blurDialog, Alert Dialog, Sheet, Command
::details-contentJS measured height + animationAccordion, Collapsible
:popover-openJS class toggling for open stateAll popover components
:indeterminateJS class for indeterminate stateProgress, Checkbox
CSS Typography & Form
text-wrap: balanceJS text-balancing librariesTypography (headings)
text-wrap: prettyManual &nbsp; for orphan preventionTypography (body text)
field-sizing: contentJS auto-resize scriptsTextarea, Input
hanging-punctuationNegative text-indent hacksBlockquotes, pull quotes
CSS Scrolling
scroll-snapJS snap-to-slide calculationsCarousel
overscroll-behavior: containJS scroll-lock librariesCommand, Dropdown, Sheet, Scroll Area
scrollbar-gutter: stablePadding hacks for scrollbar widthCommand, Scroll Area
CSS Architecture
CSS nesting / @layerSass, Less, PostCSSAll components
@scopeBEM naming, Shadow DOM, CSS modulesScoped component styles
@supportsModernizr, JS feature detectionProgressive enhancement
@media (scripting)<noscript> onlyNo-JS fallbacks
CSS math (clamp/min/max/round)JS responsive calculationsFluid typography, responsive spacing
content-visibilityJS lazy renderingAccordion, Collapsible, Tree View
CSS Accessibility
prefers-reduced-motionNo motion accommodationAll animated components (critical for a11y)
prefers-color-schemeJS-only dark mode detectionAuto dark mode
prefers-contrastNo contrast accommodationAll components (high contrast support)
forced-colorsBroken styles in Windows High ContrastAll components (system color fallbacks)
JavaScript APIs
Web Animations APICSS class toggling for sequenced animationsToast
Intl.DateTimeFormatmoment.js, date-fnsCalendar
Intl.NumberFormatnumeral.js, manual formattingStatistic, Number Input
Intl.RelativeTimeFormattimeago.js, moment .fromNow()Timeline, activity feeds
Intl.ListFormatManual join/comma logicTag lists, multi-select displays
Drag and Drop APISortableJS, drag librariesSortable
CustomEventFramework event busesCalendar, inter-component communication
checkVisibility()Manual offset/dimension calculationsTree View
IntersectionObserverScroll listeners + getBoundingClientRect()Lazy loading, infinite scroll, reveal effects
ResizeObserverWindow resize listeners + pollingResponsive components, Carousel
MutationObserverPolling loops, framework lifecycleAuto-init for dynamically added components
Clipboard APIexecCommand('copy')Copy buttons, share links, code blocks
CloseWatcherManual Escape key listenersCustom overlays, full-screen views
ElementInternalsHidden <input> proxy patternsCustom form controls (Web Components)
FormDataManual value collection loopsForm, search, filters
AbortControllerBoolean flags, manual cleanupSearch-as-you-type, debounced fetches
structuredClone()lodash cloneDeep, JSON round-tripState management, undo/redo
Navigation APIHistory API hacks, router librariesSPA router, pagination
Selection APIRange/offset calculationsText editors, annotation features
View Transitions API (JS)DOM-diffing + class-toggled crossfadesSPA navigation, content swaps

These APIs are supported in the latest versions of Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Safari. Some newer features (Invoker Commands, CSS Anchor Positioning, ::details-content, field-sizing, interpolate-size, View Transitions, Scroll-Driven Animations) may have varying cross-browser support — check Baseline for current compatibility status and each component's component-skill.md for the specific APIs used.
